summary: Collagen accounts for approximately 30% of total body protein and has a characteristic triple helix structure.
Collagen accounts for approximately 10% of total body protein and has a beta-sheet structure.
Collagen accounts for approximately 50% of total body protein and has an alpha helix structure.
Every third amino acid in collagen is glycine, the smallest amino acid, allowing tight triple helix winding.
Every third amino acid in collagen is alanine, which provides hydrophobic stability.
Every third amino acid in collagen is proline, which introduces rigid kinks in the chain.
Vitamin C is essential for hydroxylation of proline and lysine in collagen synthesis; its deficiency causes scurvy.
Vitamin D is essential for collagen cross-linking; its deficiency causes rickets.
Vitamin K is required for collagen hydroxylation; its deficiency causes bleeding.
Osteogenesis imperfecta results from mutations in Type I collagen genes.
Osteogenesis imperfecta results from mutations in Type III collagen genes.
Osteogenesis imperfecta results from deficiency of vitamin C affecting collagen synthesis.
Vascular Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is caused by defective Type III collagen.
Vascular Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is caused by defective Type I collagen.
Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is caused by deficiency of lysyl oxidase enzyme.
The basement membrane contains Type IV collagen arranged in a sheet-like network.
The basement membrane contains Type I collagen arranged in fibrils.
The basement membrane contains elastin fibres for structural support.
Fibronectin connects cells to the extracellular matrix through integrin receptors.
Fibronectin connects cells through cadherin receptors at tight junctions.
Fibronectin is the main structural protein of tendons and ligaments.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review extracellular matrix concepts and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent understanding of the extracellular matrix!
